hypothesis: Among the Si–X alloy candidates identified by the AI framework as exhibiting invariant Li⁺ migration channel sizes across lithiation states, those whose stabilizing alloying element (X) forms a rigid, electronegatively distinct sublattice that is thermodynamically decoupled from the Si–Li alloying reaction will demonstrate a quantifiable threshold effect: below a critical molar fraction of X (predicted to lie between 5–15 at%), channel radius variance will scale linearly with X content, but above this threshold, channel variance will plateau near zero regardless of further X addition, a nonlinear relationship that can be experimentally verified through in-situ synchrotron X-ray diffraction combined with electrochemical cycling and computationally tested by systematically varying X concentration in CHGNet-relaxed structures while measuring the slope of channel radius versus state of charge.
